commit.message:
fix: improve ARIA accessibility and remove proposal sentiment auth friction (#182)

Add comprehensive ARIA attributes and focus-visible rings across 17 high-traffic
components to move toward WCAG 2.1 AA compliance:

- ScoreRing: add role="meter" with aria-valuenow/min/max/label
- ScoreCard: add role="group" and role="img" to pillar status dots
- CivicaDRepCard/CivicaSPOCard: add aria-label, focus-visible rings, sr-only trend labels
- SPO pillar bars: add role="progressbar" with full aria attributes
- CrossChainRadar: add aria-label, focus/blur handlers, focus-visible to legend buttons
- ScoreImpactChip: add role="status" and aria-label
- ShareModal: add aria-labels and aria-hidden to decorative icons
- CivicaHeader: add focus-visible to nav links, home link, user menu trigger
- CivicaBottomNav: add focus-visible rings and aria-label to links
- CommandPalette: add aria-hidden to keyboard shortcut footer
- CivicaPulseOverview: add role="tablist"/"tab"/"tabpanel" with aria-selected/controls
- StatCard: add focus-visible and aria-label for linked cards
- QuickMatchFlow: add aria-pressed/label to question buttons, aria-hidden to icons,
  focus-visible to radio buttons and result cards
- ProposalSentiment: soften unconnected user CTA, add aria-label to card, focus-visible
  to vote buttons and change-vote link
- ConcernFlags: add aria-label to card, focus-visible to flag buttons
- ConcernFlagBanner: add role="alert" and aria-label
- PrioritySignals: add focus-visible to all interactive buttons, progressbar roles
- EngagementSummary: add aria-label to summary card

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add test suite — 51 tests covering API, checks, and Email Health page

Includes pytest.ini config, conftest.py with mocked DNS fixtures,
test_checks.py (20 tests for all 13 check functions), and
test_api.py (31 tests for routes, static pages, sidebar consistency,
and Email Health page structure).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: implement offline node state recovery and catch-up (#532)

* feat: implement offline node state recovery and catch-up

When a node comes back online after a long outage where its peers have
compacted the event log, it now detects this and recovers via a full
snapshot rather than silently missing events.

Changes:
- types.ts: add ClusterSnapshot and CatchUpProgress interfaces
- events.ts: add snapshot_version_vector column to cluster_state (backward-
  compat ALTER TABLE), add getSnapshotVersionVector / setSnapshotVersionVector /
  getEffectiveVersionVector so delta requests skip events already in a snapshot
- replication.ts: export the three new event functions
- raft-state-machine.ts: add isCatchingUp flag; startElectionLoop suppresses
  elections (and resets deadline) while the node is catching up
- cluster-http-server.ts: add GET /cluster/v1/snapshot endpoint and
  getSnapshot handler to ClusterHttpHandlers
- runtime.ts: add cachedSnapshot (refreshed on every sync), buildSnapshot(),
  applySnapshot(), requestSnapshot(), isDeltaInsufficient(), and
  recoverFromSnapshot(); pullEventsFromPeers now returns structured result
  including catch-up progress; getEffectiveVersionVector used for delta
  requests; ClusterSyncResult and ClusterStatus extended with new fields
- state-recovery.test.ts: 18 new tests covering snapshot vector management,
  effective version vector, catching-up election suppression, delta
  sufficiency detection, snapshot application, and the HTTP endpoint
- distributed-runtime-coverage.test.ts: update existing sync-metrics test
  for new ClusterSyncResult shape

Story: STORY-STATE-RECOVERY

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove unused imports from state-recovery test

fix: individual task pipeline parity with campaign tasks

- Blog gate (index.js): block task_completion.approve for blog_post/article/newsletter
  tasks that complete peer review without a linked document — reset to in-progress
  and inject a system comment directing the agent to use create-doc + submit-for-publish

- Triage hint (tasks.html): Nova triage hint now updates dynamically based on selected
  task type, showing the expected agent and pipeline for each type

- Nova prompt (prompt-builders.js): added explicit per-type triage rules for individual
  (non-campaign) tasks so Nova assigns to the correct agent with the right pipeline
  instructions; blog tasks explicitly warned never to use execute-task

- CEO-assigned bypass (agent-runner.js): individual tasks where CEO set an explicit
  assignee + dueDate now bypass the Nova triage gate in _triagedIdle — agents can
  pick them up immediately without waiting for a Nova comment

- ops/bug_fix/editorial auto-complete (index.js): added to _INTERNAL_TASK_TYPES so
  they auto-complete after peer review like research/general, consistent with how
  campaign tasks of these types behave

- Social copy URL injection (agent-runner.js): when no campaign context is available,
  Scribe copy task now scans the task description and Echo's strategy brief for a
  specific ambientpixels.ai URL before falling back to the generic site URL

feat(ci): wire Google OAuth credentials into api container via .env (#203)

* feat(admin-auth): backend foundation — DB migration, role/permission model, admin config

- V2 Flyway migration: admin_users, admin_sessions, admin_mfa_backup_codes tables
  with brute-force lockout fields (failed_attempts, locked_until) and MFA scaffold
- AdminRole enum (ADMIN, OPERATOR, FINANCE, AUDITOR, HELPDESK)
- AdminPermissions: 37 atomic permissions with role→permission matrix + check() / requirePermission()
- AdminModels: AdminLoginRequest/Response, AdminUserResponse, AdminCreate/UpdateUserRequest
- AppConfig: adminJwtSecret (HS256), adminJwtIssuer, adminAccessTokenTtlMs, adminRefreshTokenTtlDays
- build.gradle.kts: add at.favre.lib:bcrypt:0.10.2 for admin password hashing

https://claude.ai/code/session_018LvGLRUNYfVnPWs8sALLQm

* feat(admin-auth): backend auth service, routes, and DI wiring

- AdminAuthService: bcrypt password verify (at.favre.lib:bcrypt), brute-force lockout
  (5 attempts → 15-min lock), single-use refresh token rotation via SHA-256 hash in DB,
  JWT HS256 issuance/verification, admin user CRUD
- AdminAuthRoutes: POST /admin/auth/{login,refresh,logout}, GET /admin/auth/me,
  GET/POST/PATCH /admin/users, DELETE /admin/users/{id}/sessions
  — httpOnly SameSite=Strict cookies; refresh cookie narrowed to /admin/auth path
- Authentication.kt: added admin-jwt-hs256 named config alongside existing jwt-rs256
- Routing.kt: mount adminAuthRoutes() under auth rate-limit tier
- AppModule.kt: register AdminAuthService(get()) in Koin DI
- V2 migration: timestamps changed to BIGINT epoch-ms (consistent with Exposed long mapping)

https://claude.ai/code/session_018LvGLRUNYfVnPWs8sALLQm

* feat(admin-auth): frontend auth store, api-client, and use-auth replacement

- auth-store.ts: Zustand store for auth state (user, isLoading); not persisted —
  hydrated on every load via /admin/auth/me query
- api/auth.ts: TanStack Query hooks — useCurrentUser() (GET /me), useAdminLogin()
  (POST /login), useAdminLogout() (POST /logout)
- use-auth.ts: replaced CF JWT decoding with auth-store reads; 37-permission matrix
  mirrors AdminPermissions.kt (ADMIN/OPERATOR/FINANCE/AUDITOR/HELPDESK roles)
- api-client.ts: replaced CF-Access-Token header with credentials:'include' (httpOnly
  cookies sent automatically); 401 hook redirects to /login
- constants.ts: removed CF_COOKIE_NAME; added ROUTES.LOGIN
- types/user.ts: aligned AdminRole (ADMIN/OPERATOR/FINANCE/AUDITOR/HELPDESK) and
  AdminUser fields with backend AdminUserResponse schema

https://claude.ai/code/session_018LvGLRUNYfVnPWs8sALLQm

- routes/login.tsx: ZyntaPOS branded login page with email+password form;
  react-hook-form + zod validation; show/hide password toggle; mapped server
  errors (401 invalid creds, 429 locked, 403 inactive); redirects to / on success
- routes/__root.tsx: auth guard — calls useCurrentUser() on every load to hydrate
  auth state from httpOnly cookie; redirects unauthenticated users to /login;

- login.tsx: move useForm() before early return to satisfy

https://claude.ai/code/session_018LvGLRUNYfVnPWs8sALLQm

* fix(admin): update user components for new AdminRole/AdminUser types

- UserTable: update ROLE_LABELS/ROLE_COLORS to 5-role set, replace

  lastLoginAt (inline formatEpoch helper replaces formatRelativeTime)

https://claude.ai/code/session_018LvGLRUNYfVnPWs8sALLQm

* feat(core): add AppTimezone singleton for system-wide timezone config

- AppTimezone.kt: new singleton in :shared:core holding the active
  IANA timezone (default Asia/Colombo). Exposes set(tzId) for runtime
  updates and current: TimeZone for consumers.
- DateTimeUtils.kt + LongExtensions.kt: all default tz parameters now
  use AppTimezone.current instead of TimeZone.currentSystemDefault(),
  so every date/time display throughout the KMP app automatically
  respects the store-configured timezone.
- ReportRepositoryImpl.kt: tz field changed from val to a computed
  property (get() = AppTimezone.current) so report date-boundary
  queries always use the live configured timezone.
- SettingsViewModel.kt: calls AppTimezone.set() after loadGeneral()
  reads the stored timezone and again immediately after saveGeneral()
  persists a new value — no app restart required.

https://claude.ai/code/session_018LvGLRUNYfVnPWs8sALLQm

* feat(admin): timezone store, preferences page, and timezone-aware display

- timezone-store.ts: Zustand store with localStorage persistence
  (key: zynta-admin-prefs). Holds IANA timezone string defaulting
  to Asia/Colombo. Ships a curated TIMEZONE_OPTIONS list of 33 zones.
- utils.ts: add formatEpochDateTime / formatEpochDate / formatEpochRelative
  helpers that accept epoch-ms + IANA tz string and use Intl.DateTimeFormat.
  Also adds tzOffsetLabel() to render "UTC+05:30" offsets in the UI.
- use-timezone.ts: hook returning { timezone, formatDateTime, formatDate,
  formatRelative } — pre-bound to the current store timezone so components
  don't pass the timezone explicitly.
- routes/settings/index.tsx: new Preferences page at /settings with a
  searchable timezone selector (33 options). Saves to Zustand/localStorage
  on "Save Preferences".
- routeTree.gen.ts: register /settings/ route.
- Sidebar.tsx: add "Preferences" nav item (SlidersHorizontal icon) under
  new "Account" group linking to /settings.
- UserTable.tsx: replace inline formatEpoch() with useTimezone().formatRelative

https://claude.ai/code/session_018LvGLRUNYfVnPWs8sALLQm

* fix(core): fix AppTimezone init order and missing import in LongExtensions

https://claude.ai/code/session_018LvGLRUNYfVnPWs8sALLQm

* fix(admin): fix infinite loading spinner when /me returns 401

auth-store starts with isLoading=true. When useCurrentUser's queryFn
threw on 401, neither setUser nor clearUser was called, so isLoading
stayed true forever and the full-screen spinner never went away.

Fix: catch the error in queryFn, call clearUser() to clear isLoading,
then re-throw so react-query still tracks the error state. throwOnError:false
ensures the error doesn't propagate to the error boundary.

Backend:
- GET /admin/auth/status → { needsBootstrap: bool } (no auth required)
- POST /admin/auth/bootstrap → creates first ADMIN user; 409 if any admin exists
- AdminModels.kt: AdminBootstrapRequest + AdminStatusResponse data classes
- AdminAuthService: needsBootstrap() + bootstrap() methods

Frontend (admin-panel):
- useAdminStatus() and useAdminBootstrap() hooks in api/auth.ts
- /setup/ page: full-screen form (name, email, password) with validation,
  password visibility toggle, and server-error handling; redirects to /login on success
- __root.tsx: checks needsBootstrap on every load; redirects to /setup/ if true,
  treats /setup as a public page alongside /login
- routeTree.gen.ts: SetupIndexRoute registered across all route type interfaces

Fixes chicken-and-egg on fresh deploy — no seed admin needed in migrations.

Private top-level functions are file-scoped in Kotlin — all 7 new admin
route files call resolveAdminUser() from AdminAuthRoutes.kt, causing a
compile error. Changed private → internal so they are accessible within
the same module while remaining package-private from outside.

     DoS via bcrypt's silent 72-byte truncation behaviour. Applied to login,
     bootstrap, and createUser validation.

G3 — Wire AdminAuditService into AdminAuthService (DI: AppModule updated).
     LOGIN_SUCCESS, LOGIN_FAILURE, ADMIN_LOGIN_LOCKOUT, ADMIN_TOKEN_REFRESHED,
     ADMIN_LOGOUT, ADMIN_SESSIONS_REVOKED, and ADMIN_PASSWORD_CHANGED events
     now written to admin_audit_log on every auth action.

     isActive filters; returns AdminPagedResponse<AdminUserResponse>. GET
     /admin/users route updated to forward query params.

G1 — CSRF plugin (Csrf.kt) created and wired into Routing.kt via
     withCsrfProtection{}. Reads XSRF-TOKEN cookie; validates X-XSRF-Token
     header on POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE. Frontend api-client.ts updated to read
     XSRF-TOKEN cookie and attach X-XSRF-Token header automatically.

G5 — New settings/profile.tsx: change-password form + active sessions list
     with Revoke All button.

G6 — UserTable.tsx: MFA status column (ShieldCheck/ShieldOff) + Revoke Sessions
     action in dropdown. useRevokeSessions hook added to api/users.ts.

New endpoints:
  GET  /admin/users/{id}/sessions  — list active sessions
  POST /admin/auth/change-password — change own password (revokes all sessions)

G8 — Backend unit tests:

  CsrfPluginTest — cookie issuance, 403 on missing/wrong header, excluded paths

* feat(api): implement /v1/products and /v1/sync push/pull with real DB logic

POS sync HTTP endpoints were placeholder stubs returning empty responses.
This commit wires them to PostgreSQL via Exposed ORM.

ProductService:
- Define Products Exposed table (mirrors V1 schema)
- list() queries products table filtered by storeId + optional updatedSince
- Returns PagedResponse<ProductDto> with pagination and hasMore flag

SyncService:
- push(): upserts operations into sync_queue (idempotent on retry)
  For PRODUCT entity type, applies LWW upsert/soft-delete to products table
- pull(): queries sync_queue for ops with vectorClock > since, returns
  up to limit operations ordered ASC with hasMore detection

feat(ops): add backend/scripts — backup, restore, verify, WAL archive, kuma setup

backup.sh (enhanced): GPG encryption + rclone B2 upload + Kuma push heartbeat
restore.sh (new): download from B2 (optional), decrypt GPG, restore to DB
verify-backup.sh (new): restore latest backup to temp DB, compare row counts
archive-wal.sh (new): PostgreSQL WAL segment upload to B2 (called by archive_command)
setup-uptime-kuma.sh (new): creates all 19 Uptime Kuma monitors + alert channels
  via REST API — run once after FTS-5 to configure status.zyntapos.com

Closes audit gaps: P0 (POS sync), P1 (backup scripts, Uptime Kuma automation)

* fix(ci): avoid secrets context in step if-condition inside reusable workflow

GitHub Actions rejects workflow_call workflows that reference the secrets
context inside step-level if: conditions. Move the empty-check into the
shell script using an env var instead.

* fix(api): fix ProductService timestamp comparison — import SqlExpressionBuilder.greaterEq

timestampWithTimeZone columns require explicit SqlExpressionBuilder import for
comparison operators. Changed 'greater' to 'greaterEq' (returns products updated
at or after the given timestamp, semantically correct for sync incremental pull).

https://claude.ai/code/session_018LvGLRUNYfVnPWs8sALLQm

* fix(api): mount admin_jwt_secret.txt and set ADMIN_JWT_SECRET_FILE env var

API container was crashing at startup with IllegalStateException because
ADMIN_JWT_SECRET_FILE was not configured. Generate secret on VPS:
  openssl rand -hex 32 > secrets/admin_jwt_secret.txt

https://claude.ai/code/session_018LvGLRUNYfVnPWs8sALLQm

* fix(ci): generate admin_jwt_secret.txt with 644 permissions in FTS step 4

The admin JWT secret file was missing from FTS step 4 generation and had
to be created manually. When created via shell redirection, it defaulted
to 600 (owner-only) which Docker containers running as non-root couldn't
read, causing AppConfig.fromEnvironment() to throw ADMIN_JWT_SECRET must
be set on every startup.

- Add admin_jwt_secret.txt generation to FTS step 4 (idempotent)
- Ensure chmod 644 so Docker bind-mount is readable by container process
- Also chmod 644 in the final permission-hardening block (remediation)

https://claude.ai/code/session_018LvGLRUNYfVnPWs8sALLQm

* feat(ci): wire Google OAuth credentials into api container via .env

Add GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID/SECRET/REDIRECT_URI env vars to docker-compose.yml
api service (read from VPS .env via ${VAR:-} substitution). Update
vps-update-env.yml to sync Google OAuth GitHub Secrets to the VPS .env
file alongside Sentry DSNs.

Also fix vps-update-env restart target: was 'zyntapos-api' (wrong service
name), now 'api' to match the docker-compose.yml service name.

commit.message:
fix: handle INITIAL_SESSION event in CreatePasswordPage

Supabase replays current auth state as INITIAL_SESSION when onAuthStateChange
is first subscribed. If the invite hash was processed before subscription,
SIGNED_IN is never re-fired — only INITIAL_SESSION with the session.
Also use a ref for the timeout check to avoid stale closure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: pass PKG_CONFIG environment variables directly to uv on macOS

The export statements don't persist when the script is piped via curl,
so we need to pass the environment variables directly to the uv command.
This ensures pycairo can find cairo during the build process.

exercised through both concrete subclasses
(`SetExplicitMockBehaviorAnalyzer` and `SetStrictMockBehaviorAnalyzer`)

and `AnalyzeInvocation` method guards

assembly)

fix: enable scroll boundary fix on Android (not just iOS) (#665)

The useIOSScrollFix hook only activated on iOS devices, leaving Android
Chrome vulnerable to the same overscroll-contain scroll-lock bug. Remove
the isIOS guard since the touchstart handler is naturally a no-op on
desktop (mouse never fires touchstart).

commit.message:
fix(auth): dedicated login route that sets cookies server-side

Creates /api/auth/login route.ts (takes priority over [...path] catch-all)
that calls the backend server-side (no CORS) and sets the nz_access_token
cookie via NextResponse.cookies.set() from a Node.js serverless context
(not Edge). This correctly sets Domain=.balizero.com cookies.

Reverts the direct-backend fetch approach that broke due to CORS preflight.
Also fixes PrimeMap3D console.error and null check.

commit.message:
Add 21 integration test scenarios to all non-Go packages

Ports all 21 end-to-end integration scenarios from the Go reference
implementation to Python, TypeScript, .NET, and Rust. Scenarios cover
audit trails, reputation, consent, governance, supply chain (EGIP),
research integrity, creator provenance, family decisions, knowledge
verification, AI ethics, agent identity, community lifecycle, system
evolution, sprint lifecycle, marketplace disputes, community evolution,
agent lifecycle, whistleblow/recall, emergency response, knowledge
ecosystems, and constitutional schism.

Test counts: Python 427, TypeScript 425, .NET 367, Rust 321.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: blochAngles single-pass reduce + vqe all-identity fast path

- blochAngles: three-loop pattern had rho11 computed, reset, recomputed.
  Replaced with a single pass: rho11 accumulates on qubit-q=1 entries,
  rho00/rho01 on qubit-q=0 entries.
- vqe: [...ops].every(ch => ch === 'I') allocated a throwaway array.
  Replaced with !/[XYZ]/.test(ops) — direct regex on the string.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: use libtmux environment param instead of env command prefix

Pass per-agent env vars (AGENT_ID, WEB_BASE_URL, API_KEY) via
libtmux's new_window/split ``environment`` parameter rather than
prefixing the launch command with ``env VAR=value``.

Maps to ``tmux new-window -e KEY=VALUE`` / ``split-window -e KEY=VALUE``
which are pane-local — no race condition, no shell command injection.

- TmuxInterface.new_pane(environment=...) and new_subpane(environment=...)
- ClaudeCodeAgent.start() builds pane_env dict and passes to new_pane/new_subpane
- Remove _set_session_env_vars() (session-level env vars no longer used)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix mobile UI: prevent horizontal scroll, enlarge touch targets (#16)

* Fix mobile UI: prevent horizontal scroll, enlarge touch targets

- Add maximum-scale=1.0 and user-scalable=no to viewport meta to prevent
  accidental pinch-zoom on the PWA
- Increase item edit/delete buttons to 44x44px minimum touch targets
  (were ~24px with only 4px padding)
- Increase .btn-sm to proper tap size (44px min-height) on mobile
- Add touch-action: manipulation globally to prevent double-tap-zoom delay
- Fix label grid to 2 columns on small screens (was 3, causing overflow)
- Add overflow-x: hidden on main content area
- Allow item descriptions to wrap on mobile instead of forcing nowrap
- Enlarge back button, image modal close button, and nav bar touch targets
- Add flex-wrap to sync status rows and print controls for narrow screens

https://claude.ai/code/session_01BiHfwuGJ9Mej3FQT8yvDHC

* Increase edit/delete icon sizes for better visibility on mobile

Edit icon: 18px → 24px, delete icon: 20px → 26px. The 44px touch
target was correct but the rendered Unicode glyphs were still too
small to see and aim for comfortably.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01BiHfwuGJ9Mej3FQT8yvDHC

* Double edit/delete icon sizes (edit: 24→36px, delete: 26→40px)

https://claude.ai/code/session_01BiHfwuGJ9Mej3FQT8yvDHC
